steps.title: Identify the sentence's function
steps.content: The first sentence has the structure of a definition: “Yawn contagion occurs when...” It states what the phenomenon is, not what problem needs solving, what hypothesis needs testing, or what claim needs challenging. The definitional structure is the decisive signal.

steps.title: Match the function to the answer
steps.content: The sentence defines yawn contagion, and the rest of the passage discusses this defined phenomenon through research findings—studies in primates, wild vs. captive populations, and patterns by gender and social group. Choices B, C, and D mischaracterize the first sentence: it doesn't introduce a problem to examine, challenge a claim, or present a hypothesis to evaluate. That matches choice A — A is correct.
